user_title: Head of Change Execution, WPB Private Banking & Wealth Solutions, Asia Pacific

talking_points.headline: HSBC Q1 Results: Asia NNM Doubles to $34bn — Wealth Strategy Confirmed, Execution Gap Next
talking_points.context_html: HSBC’s Q1 2026 results confirm the APAC wealth strategy is delivering: net new money surged to $39bn ($34bn from Asia) versus $23bn a year ago, with wealth balances up 12% to $1.57tn — a direct validation of the change programmes Adam’s teams have been executing. Group revenue of $18.6bn (+6%) beat estimates and Georges Elhedery, Group CEO of HSBC described a “simple, more agile, growing HSBC,” but profit marginally missed consensus as credit losses rose; Pam Kaur, Group CFO of HSBC stated cost savings will be “more apparent in H2 2026,” placing the full execution burden on Adam’s change delivery teams for the next two quarters.
source_links: CNBC: HSBC Q1 Profit Misses on ECL
Asian Banker: Wealth Fees Drive Q1

talking_points.headline: Citi’s Arc Sets the Enterprise AI Agents Benchmark — HSBC APAC Teams Must Now Respond
talking_points.context_html: Citi launched Arc on 3 May — an enterprise AI agents platform deploying fully governed, auditable agents across its entire global workforce for research, synthesis, client preparation and execution workflows. This is a direct competitive signal for Adam’s AI-enabled change mandate in APAC: a tier-one peer has moved decisively past pilot programmes into enterprise-wide governed deployment, with wealth management the lead use case — client meeting prep automated from hours to minutes. Adam’s change teams need to define HSBC’s equivalent roadmap, with Arc now the reference architecture for what “at-scale AI delivery” looks like in global private banking.
source_links: Banking Dive: Citi Launches Arc Platform
PYMNTS: Citi Debuts Arc for Banking

talking_points.headline: Mythos AI Warning Adds a Mandatory Security Gate to Every APAC AI Change Delivery
talking_points.context_html: Scott Bessent, US Treasury Secretary and Jerome Powell, Chair of the Federal Reserve summoned major bank CEOs on 3 May to warn that Anthropic’s Mythos model is a “step change” in AI capability, having identified thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities across bank systems; banks were told to use it offensively before adversaries do. For Adam’s change execution teams deploying AI-powered wealth and banking features across APAC, this is a mandatory new gate: adversarial AI security testing must be embedded in every delivery cycle before any AI feature reaches production. The warning directly affects the timeline and governance of every AI initiative currently in flight.
source_links: Bloomberg: Banks Gird Against AI Attacks

HSBC News.title: HSBC Q1 2026 — Wealth NNM Doubles to $39bn, Revenue Beats, Profit Misses
HSBC News.summary: HSBC reported Q1 group revenue of $18.6bn (+6% YoY, beat estimates), WPB wealth NNM surged to $39bn ($34bn from Asia) vs $23bn a year ago, and wealth balances rose 12% to $1.57tn; pre-tax profit of $9.37bn slightly missed consensus as credit losses rose 49%, while cost savings track 6 months ahead of schedule.

AI & Technology.title: Citi Launches Arc — Enterprise AI Agents Platform Across Global Banking Workforce
AI & Technology.summary: Citi deployed Arc on 3 May, an enterprise AI agents platform enabling governed, auditable agentic AI across its entire global workforce — becoming the first major bank to launch such a platform at scale, with immediate wealth management applications in RM client preparation.

AI & Technology.title: Compliant Messaging (WhatsApp/WeChat) Is APAC Wealth's Overlooked Retention Differentiator
AI & Technology.summary: HSBC is specifically named as pursuing WhatsApp and WeChat connectivity for APAC wealth advisors — compliant messaging has become a primary HNW client retention differentiator in Asia, with firms that delay rollout risking attrition to more digitally agile rivals.

Regulatory & Markets.title: Bessent & Powell Warn Banks: Anthropic Mythos AI Exposed Thousands of Bank Vulnerabilities
Regulatory & Markets.summary: US Treasury Secretary Bessent and Fed Chair Powell urgently convened bank CEOs to warn that Anthropic's Mythos AI is a step-change capability that identified thousands of high-severity bank vulnerabilities, urging firms to use it offensively to find their own weaknesses before adversaries do.

Regulatory & Markets.title: HKIA: Hong Kong Gross Premiums Hit $105.6bn in 2025, Up 29.7% YoY
Regulatory & Markets.summary: The Hong Kong Insurance Authority reported $105.6bn gross premiums in 2025 (+29.7% YoY), with long-term business assets at $689bn — validating HSBC Life's insurance-linked wealth strategy and providing market context for the Euromoney 2026 Best Insurance Provider for Wealth Management win.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Standard Chartered Q1 2026 Full Figures — $443bn Wealth AUM, 17.4% RoTE, EPS +31%
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Standard Chartered reported $1.7bn net profit in Q1 2026 (+18% YoY), wealth AUM at $443bn ($233bn wealth + $210bn deposits), 17.4% RoTE and EPS +31%, with wealth income growing strongly — completing the peer competitor scorecard on the same day as HSBC results day.
